metadata.title: BC Building Code Amendments to NBC 2020 - Part 4-8
metadata.description: British Columbia-specific amendments for Part 4-8 (Structural Design)
metadata.author: BC Building Code Team

amendment.description: Replace ASME A17.1 reference with Safety Standards Act in Table 4.1.5.11

entry.note: See the Safety Standards Act and pursuant regulations.

amendment.description: Replace ASME A17.1 reference with Safety Standards Act in Table 4.1.8.18

row.entry: Elevators and escalatorsSee also the Safety Standards Act and pursuant regulations.

amendment.description: Add CSA A344.2 reference for steel pallet storage racks in Article 4.1.8.18.(13)

new-content.text: Free-standing steel pallet storage racks are permitted to be designed according to CSA A344.2, "Standard for design and construction of steel storage racks," and to resist earthquake effects using rational analysis, provided design achieves the minimum performance level required by (See .)

amendment.description: Replace NPC and NBC reference with BC Building Code in Note A-4.1.6.4.(3)

find-replace.find: The designer must ensure that the building complies with both the NPC and the NBC.
find-replace.replace: The designer must ensure that the building complies with the BC Building Code Book I and Book II: Plumbing Systems

amendment.description: Replace NBC with British Columbia Building Code in Article 5.1.5.1
Replace NPC reference with BC Book II reference in Article 5.6.2.2.(2)

sentence.text: Where precipitation can accumulate on sloped or horizontal assemblies, provision shall be made for drainage conforming with Article 2.4.10.4. of Division B of Book II (Plumbing Systems) of this Code.

amendment.description: Replace wired glass assembly text with fire-protection rating in Article 5.9.2.1.(3)

sentence.text: Windows, doors and skylights, including their components, that are required to have a fire-protection rating need not conform to the requirements of this Subsection. (See .)

amendment.description: Add exception and components text to sentence in Article 5.9.2.2.(1)

new-content.text: Except as permitted in and , windows, doors, skylights, including their components, shall conform to the requirements in

amendment.description: Add exception text to clause (b) in Article 5.9.2.2.(1)
Add new sentence (4) about loads and procedures in Article 5.9.2.2

sentence.text: For the purposes of conformance with , loads and procedures from may be used instead of the loads and procedures set out in the standard. (See Note 5.9.2.2.(4).)

amendment.description: Add new sentence (5) about Limited Water doors in Article 5.9.2.2

sentence.text: A door designated as a "Limited Water" door in accordance with the standard referenced in shall not be used unless the door

clause.text: separates a dwelling unit from an unconditioned storage garage or a carport,
is designed with a clear width, a clear and level space, a door-opening device and a door closer in conformance with (see .), or
meets the criteria in such that flashing would not be required.

amendment.description: Simplify sentence structure for non-conforming products in Article 5.9.2.3.(1)

sentence.text: Windows, doors, skylights and their components that do not conform to shall be designed and constructed in accordance with , and (See .)

amendment.description: Add sentence (2) about fire-protection rating in Article 5.9.3.1

sentence.text: Other fenestration assemblies and their components that are required to have a fire-protection rating need not conform to the requirements of this Subsection. (See .)

amendment.description: Replace NBC with code in Note A-5.1.4.1.(2)

find-replace.find: may impact compliance with other Parts of the NBC:
find-replace.replace: may impact compliance with other Parts of the code:

amendment.description: Replace NBC with British Columbia Building Code in Note A-5.1.4.2

new-content.paragraph: It is also noted that contains requirements for actions beyond the scope of the British Columbia Building Code, which may not be the responsibility of the designer, builder or authority having jurisdiction. These include requirements relating to quality assurance, inspection, maintenance, minimum design service lives and potential impacts of climate change, which are not addressed in the Code. The reference herein to is not intended to imply that the designer, builder or authority having jurisdiction adopt, apply or enforce any of these requirements.

amendment.description: Replace NBC with code in Note A-5.2.2.2.(4)

new-content.paragraph: The wind uplift resistance obtained from the test method in is limited to configurations with specific fastener or adhesive patterns. To extrapolate the test data to non-tested configurations, refer to for a rational calculation procedure. However, in using this extrapolation procedure, wind loads should be calculated in accordance with the code. NRC's guide for wind design referenced above provides further guidance and examples of wind load calculations.

amendment.description: Replace national reference with BC provincial reference in Note A-5.3.1.2.(1)

find-replace.find: For most of the country, however, exterior design temperatures are much lower; for example, -20°C in Toronto and Charlottetown, and -50°C in Dawson.
find-replace.replace: In other areas of the Province, however, exterior design temperatures are much lower.

amendment.description: Replace Note A-5.9.2.1.(3) with broader scope covering all fenestration assemblies

application-note.title: Airtightness and Watertightness of Windows, Doors, Skylights, Other Fenestration Assemblies and their Components Required to have a Fire-Protection Rating
application-note.paragraph: The airtightness and watertightness requirements are waived for these products when used in such an application, in recognition of the fact that the availability of assemblies that meet both the requirements of the applicable standards and the requirements for fire resistance may be limited. However, control of air and water leakage should not be ignored: measures should be taken to attempt to comply with applicable requirements.

amendment.description: Add ASTM E 331 and E 547 standards to Note A-5.9.3
Add sentence (2) about indoor design temperatures for residential buildings in Article 6.2.1.1

sentence.text: Indoor design temperatures for residential buildings shall be those established in .

amendment.description: Replace sentence (2) with Reserved in Article 6.2.1.2

sentence.text: Reserved

amendment.description: Replace sentence (3) with Reserved in Article 6.2.1.2

sentence.text: Reserved

amendment.description: Replace sentence (1) with BC Safety Standards Act reference in Article 6.2.1.5

sentence.text: Except as provided in and , the installation of heating and air-conditioning equipment, including mechanical refrigeration equipment, and including provisions for mounting, clearances and air supply, shall conform to the Safety Standards Act and pursuant regulations.

amendment.description: Add sentence (2) about solid-fuel burning boilers in Article 6.2.1.5

sentence.text: A solid-fuel burning boiler accepted for use under section 10 of the Safety Standards Act satisfies section 4.1 of .

amendment.description: Add sentence (5) about suite ventilation in Article 6.3.1.1

sentence.text: For suites in buildings conforming to or , the outdoor air required by shall be supplied directly to each suite by mechanical ventilation through ducting.

amendment.description: Replace National Plumbing Code reference with BC code reference in Article 6.3.2.15.(8)

new-content.text: Make-up water connections shall be equipped with backflow prevention devices that conform to Article 2.6.2.1. of Division B of Book II, (Plumbing Systems), of this code. (See .)

amendment.description: Replace National Plumbing Code reference with BC code reference in Article 6.3.2.15.(10)

find-replace.find: Clause 2.4.2.1.(1)(e) of Division B of the
find-replace.replace: Clause 2.4.2.1.(1)(e) of Division B of Book II, (Plumbing Systems), of this code

amendment.description: Replace National Plumbing Code reference with BC code reference in Article 6.3.2.16.(6)

new-content.text: Make-up water connections shall be equipped with backflow prevention devices that conform to Article 2.6.2.1. of Division B of Book II, (Plumbing Systems), of this code. (See .)

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 6.3.4.2.(3)

new-content.text: A ventilation system required by this Subsection shall be maintained in conformance with Article 5.5.4.1. of Division B of the British Columbia Fire Code.

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 6.3.4.3.(1)

sentence.text: The ventilation system for a power-ventilated enclosure required by Sentence 5.5.4.2.(1) of Division B of the British Columbia Fire Code shall

clause.text: conform to
provide continuous exhaust ventilation at an air velocity sufficient to prevent the accumulation of combustible or reactive deposits in the power-ventilated enclosure and its exhaust duct system,
confine dangerous goods vapours and particles to the area where they are generated and exhaust them to the outdoors,
not return the exhausted air to the building, and
be provided with well identified control switches that are

subclause.text: located outside of the power-ventilated enclosure, and
readily accessible in case of an emergency.

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 6.3.4.4.(1)

sentence.text: The power-ventilated enclosure required by Sentence 5.5.4.2.(1) of Division B of the British Columbia Fire Code and its exhaust duct system shall

clause.text: except as provided in and , be constructed of noncombustible materials compatible with and chemically resistant to the dangerous goods vapours and particles being exhausted, and
be provided with access doors to permit inspection and maintenance of the fan assembly and exhaust duct system.

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 6.9.1.2.(1)

new-content.text: Except as provided in , systems serving spaces that contain hazardous gases, dusts or liquids shall be designed, constructed and installed to conform to the requirements of the applicable provincial or territorial regulations or municipal bylaws or, in the absence of such regulations or bylaws, to good engineering practice such as that described in the publications of the National Fire Protection Association and in the British Columbia Fire Code. (See .)

amendment.description: Replace Article 6.9.3.1 with BC version expanding scope and requirements

sentence.text: This Article applies to every building that contains an assembly occupancy, a care occupancy with individual suites or containing sleeping rooms or bed spaces not within a suite, a residential occupancy, a business and personal services occupancy, or a mercantile occupancy, and that

clause.text: is served by or contains a fuel-burning appliance, or
contains a storage garage.

sentence.text: Carbon monoxide (CO) alarms installed in a residential occupancy or a care occupancy as required by this Article shall

clause.text: conform to ,
be equipped with an integral alarm that satisfies the audibility requirements of ,
have no disconnect switch between the overcurrent device and the CO alarm, where the CO alarm is powered by the electrical system serving the suite (see ), and
be installed as recommended by the manufacturer.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by , where a fuel-burning appliance is installed in a suite of residential occupancy or in a suite of care occupancy, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: inside each sleeping room or bed space, or
outside each sleeping room or bed space, within 5 m of each door serving a sleeping room or bed space, measured following corridors and doorways.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by , where a fuel-burning appliance serves a residential occupancy or a care occupancy and is installed in a service room that is not in a suite of residential occupancy nor in a suite of care occupancy, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: either inside each sleeping room or bed space, or if outside, within 5 m of each door serving a sleeping room or bed space, measured following corridors and doorways, in every suite of residential occupancy or suite of care occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with the service room, and
in the service room.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by , for each suite of residential occupancy or suite of care occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with a storage garage or that is adjacent to an attic or crawl space to which the storage garage is also adjacent, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: inside each sleeping room or bed space, or
outside each sleeping room or bed space, within 5 m of each bedroom door serving a sleeping room or bed space, measured following corridors and doorways.

sentence.text: CO alarms installed in an assembly occupancy, a business and personal services occupancy, or a mercantile occupancy as required by this Article shall conform to

clause.text: , notwithstanding the scope of that standard,
, notwithstanding the scope of that standard, or
good engineering practice.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by , where a fuel-burning appliance serves an assembly occupancy, business and personal services occupancy, or mercantile occupancy, a CO alarm shall be,

clause.text: where the fuel-burning appliance is part of a system that could circulate or distribute CO to a suite of assembly occupancy, business and personal services occupancy or mercantile occupancy, installed

subclause.text: on each storey of each suite that may be exposed, and
in a suite containing an assembly major occupancy, each classroom and dedicated gathering room or space, and

clause.text: installed in the room or space in which the fuel-burning appliance is located.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by , for each suite of assembly occupancy, business and personal services occupancy, or mercantile occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with either a storage garage or a service room containing a fuel-burning appliance, or that is adjacent to either an attic or crawl space to which the storage garage or a service room containing a fuel-burning appliance is also adjacent, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: on each storey of the adjacent suite,
where the adjacent suite contains an assembly major occupancy, each classroom and dedicated room or space, and
in each service room containing a fuel-burning appliance.

sentence.text: CO detectors are permitted to be installed in lieu of CO alarms required by this Article provided the CO detectors

clause.text: sound audible signals within the location they serve, as described in to , and ,
are installed in conformance with , and
form part of the fire alarm system.

amendment.description: Add row for Article 6.3.1.1.(5) in Table 6.10.1.1
Add new application note A-6.2.1.2.(1) about outdoor design conditions

application-note.title: Outdoor Design Conditions
application-note.paragraph: In the past, the practice of ventilating buildings with outdoor air assumed that the outdoor air was of better quality than the indoor air. It has become evident that the outdoor air in some areas of Canada may not be of an acceptable quality for ventilating buildings unless certain particles and gases are first removed or reduced. In order to manage the air quality of a building's indoor environment, thus reducing the potential for adverse effects on occupants' health, the quality of outdoor air for building ventilation purposes must be addressed.

amendment.description: Replace NBC with British Columbia Building Code in Note A-6.2.1.4

find-replace.find: The prime concern of the NBC is the safety of people in and around the building
find-replace.replace: The prime concern of the British Columbia Building Code is the safety of people in and around the building

amendment.description: Add new application note A-6.3.1.1.(5) about ventilation air supplied to suites

application-note.title: Ventilation Air Supplied to Suites
application-note.paragraph: The indirect supply of required outdoor ventilation air to normally occupied spaces through corridor pressurization or other indirect systems is not permitted.

amendment.description: Replace mechanically fastened with installed as recommended by manufacturer in Note A-6.9.3.1.(2)(c)

find-replace.find: Battery-powered carbon monoxide alarms are acceptable provided that they are mechanically fastened in place.
find-replace.replace: Battery-powered carbon monoxide alarms are acceptable provided that they are installed as recommended by the manufacturer.

amendment.description: Add three new application notes A-6.9.3.1.(6), (7), and (8) for carbon monoxide alarms

application-note.title: Carbon Monoxide Devices
application-note.paragraph: Although the scope of and is limited to carbon monoxide alarms for residential applications, their use may be appropriate in some other locations where they are not subject to excessive contaminants or risk of damage, such as in classrooms and meeting spaces. The designer is also given the option to follow good engineering practice. For example, some carbon monoxide devices used in storage garages may be suitable for use in other applications.

application-note.title: Spaces Served by a Fuel-Burning Appliance
application-note.paragraph: Where a fuel-burning appliance such as a furnace circulates or distributes air to a space there is the potential for leakage of combustion products into the duct system which could then circulate combustion products including carbon monoxide (CO) to that space, so a CO alarm is required to protect occupants of that space. Fuel-burning appliances such as a boiler do not have the same potential of the system circulating or distributing CO to the spaces served via the piping system. In both examples of a fuel-burning furnace and a fuel-burning boiler, a CO alarm is required in the service room containing the appliances.

application-note.title: Adjacent Suites
application-note.paragraph: Suites that share a common attic or crawl space with a storage garage or service room, as well as suites that share a common wall or floor/ceiling assembly with a storage garage or service room are considered adjacent for the application of .

amendment.description: Replace Article 7.1.2.1 with BC version referencing BC Building Code Book II

sentence.text: Every plumbing system shall be designed and installed in conformance with the British Columbia Building Code Book II (Plumbing Systems), that being the National Plumbing Code of Canada.
References to the following documents in the British Columbia Building Code Book II (Plumbing Systems) shall be replaced as follows:

clause.text: references to the National Building Code of Canada and the National Energy Code of Canada for Buildings shall be replaced with the British Columbia Building Code, and
references to the National Fire Code of Canada shall be replaced with the British Columbia Fire Code.

sentence.text: Compliance with the British Columbia Building Code Book II (Plumbing Systems) may be achieved using alternative solutions as described in Clause 1.2.1.1.(1)(b) of Division A of this Code, using the objectives and functional statements attributed to the applicable acceptable solutions in Division B of the British Columbia Building Code Book II (Plumbing Systems).

Notwithstanding , a water closet with a dual flush cycle of 6.0 L and 4.1 L or less is deemed to comply with Sentence 2.6.1.6.(3) and Table 2.6.2.6. of Division B of the British Columbia Building Code Book II (Plumbing Systems).

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 8.1.1.1.(3)

sentence.text: Fire safety at construction and demolition sites shall conform to Section 5.6. of Division B of the British Columbia Fire Code.

amendment.description: Replace National Fire Code reference with BC Fire Code in Article 8.1.1.3.(1)

sentence.text: Measures shall be taken during demolition to protect the public in conformance with Section 5.6. of Division B of the British Columbia Fire Code.
